function_explanation: Calculates the Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) between two images.
function_docstring:
def psnr(ref, img):
    """
    Calculate the Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) between two images.

    Args:
        ref (numpy.ndarray): A reference image represented as a NumPy array with arbitrary shape.
        img (numpy.ndarray): An image to compare against the reference, represented as a NumPy array with the same shape as `ref`.

    Returns:
        float: The PSNR value, indicating the quality of the image compared to the reference.
    """
usage_example:
import numpy as np

# Example images
ref_image = np.random.rand(256, 256)
img = np.random.rand(256, 256)

# Calculate PSNR
psnr_value = psnr(ref_image, img)
print(f"PSNR: {psnr_value}")
